breaking.title: Target vs target: What US hit in Iran, and what Tehran struck in response
breaking.summary: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran has collapsed after fresh military strikes. Both nations launched attacks, expanding the conflict across the Gulf region. Iran targeted US air defence systems and facilities in several Middle Eastern countries. The United States struck approximately ninety military sites across Iran. These exchanges marked the biggest breach of the truce since April.

breaking.title: Tehran launches more strikes after explosions reported in southern Iran
breaking.summary: Centcom says it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes, with the Iranian health ministry saying 14 people were killed since Tuesday.

breaking.title: West Asia war LIVE: New U.S., Iran fighting erupts ahead of Khamenei burial
breaking.summary: The renewed hostilities, triggered by tensions over the Strait of Hormuz, sparked concerns of a return to full-scale war after an April ceasefire and a June U.S.-Iran accord ended five weeks of conflict

breaking.title: Israel Threatens To Attack Iran "With Greater Force" Amid US Strikes
breaking.summary: Israel's military is ready to strike Iran. (File)

breaking.title: Putin May Escalate Ukraine War Despite Peace Efforts By Trump: Report
breaking.summary: President Vladimir Putin is rejecting calls to negotiate peace with Kyiv, with Ukraine's recent drone strikes on Russia's oil refineries and ports strengthening his resolve to keep fighting for now.

breaking.title: Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies
breaking.summary: The attacks appear to be the latest phase of Ukraine's bid to choke off supplies and routes into and out of occupied Crimea.

breaking.title: Big fall in oil, gas and cargo ships taking US-backed Hormuz route after new strikes
breaking.summary: Data shows a decline in the number of ships - many carrying oil and gas - going through the waterway after attacks this week.

breaking.title: Woman Doctor Slapped By Corporator Resigns After Colleague Quit Due To "Fear"
breaking.summary: KDMC (Kalyan-Dombivli Municipal Corporation) Chief Medical Officer Dr. Deepa Shukla, however, said doctors Vaibhav Salunkhe and Srushti Baviskar have not submitted their resignations.
